name: Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Loop
name_original: Charco del Hervidero y Azud del Mesto — circular desde El Molar
description: The Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Loop is a moderate 8.3-mile hike near Madrid, featuring a waterfall, river canyon, and historic c
region_intro: Hiking around El Molar offers diverse landscapes, featuring river canyons, waterfalls, and historical aqueducts. The region is characterized by varied terrain, including hills such as Cerro de San Pedro and Cerro de la Atalaya. Trails navigate through lush riverside vegetation and rocky areas, providing a range of experiences for outdoor enthusiasts. This area provides a network of paths suitable for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around El Molar The most popular hiking route is Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Trail, an 8.96 miles (14.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through a rocky canyon to the double Hervidero Waterfall and along the historic El Mesto Canal. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Loop, a moderate 8.34 miles (13.4 km) path. This circular route explores the waterfall and canal system within the Guadalix River canyon, offering scenic views. Local hikers also love the Route of the Aqueducts, a 9.41 miles (15.1 km) trail leading through the Guadalix River canyon and past ancient aqueducts, often completed in about 4 hours 13 minutes. Hiking around El Molar is defined by natural features like the Hervidero Waterfall, the Guadalix River canyon, and historical aqueduct systems. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate and challenging hikes. The routes in El Molar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 360 reviews. More than 1900 hikers have used komoot to explore El Molar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around El Molar?
A: There are over 100 hiking trails around El Molar, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy strolls to more challenging routes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families in El Molar?
A: Yes, El Molar offers several easy trails perfect for families. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14894471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Coso Waterfall – Becerra Waterfall loop from San Agustín del Guadalix&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 7.2 km route. Many paths leading to the Hervidero Waterfall are also considered family-friendly.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on hikes around El Molar?
A: Hiking around El Molar allows you to explore diverse natural features. A prominent highlight is the double &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; on the Guadalix River, nestled within a rocky canyon. You can also enjoy views of hills like Cerro de San Pedro and Cerro de la Atalaya, and lush riverside vegetation.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the El Molar area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379952040/hervidero-waterfall-and-el-mesto-canal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 13.4 km path exploring the waterfall and the historic canal system. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957836132/route-of-the-aqueducts" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Aqueducts&lt;/a&gt;, a 15.1 km circular hike.

Q: What historical landmarks can be found along the hiking trails?
A: The region is rich in historical elements. You can hike along the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956888425/hervidero-waterfall-and-el-mesto-canal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Mesto Canal&lt;/a&gt;, part of the Canal de Isabel II network, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957836132/route-of-the-aqueducts" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Aqueducts&lt;/a&gt;, which passes by ancient aqueducts and the Higueras Dam. The Cerro de la Atalaya also features an ancient Moorish watchtower.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hikes around El Molar?
A: The trails around El Molar vary significantly in difficulty. Approximately half of the routes are classified as easy, with another 46 moderate trails and 8 more difficult options. This means there are suitable paths for all fitness levels, from casual walkers to experienced hikers.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in El Molar?
A: The hiking routes in El Molar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 360 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the scenic beauty of the Hervidero Waterfall, and the historical aqueduct systems.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots on the trails?
A: Yes, many trails offer scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3117324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelagua Geodetic Vertex&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent views of the Sierra de Guadarrama. Additionally, the routes through the Guadalix River canyon offer picturesque vistas, especially around the Hervidero Waterfall.

Q: Can I find trails that include water features like lakes or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. Besides the Hervidero Waterfall, you can find trails that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/484448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrezuela Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features smaller bodies of water like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2859350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Cobeña&lt;/a&gt; and Laguna de Merodio, which can be explored on various routes.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in El Molar?
A: Moderate hikes in El Molar typically range from 3 to 4.5 hours. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956888425/hervidero-waterfall-and-el-mesto-canal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Trail&lt;/a&gt; takes approximately 4 hours 3 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957836132/route-of-the-aqueducts" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Aqueducts&lt;/a&gt; is around 4 hours 13 minutes.

Q: Are there any trails that feature bridges or unique structures?
A: Yes, some trails incorporate interesting structures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/293382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viñuelas Stream Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is a notable point of interest that can be found on certain routes. Additionally, the ancient aqueducts themselves are impressive historical structures to discover.
tips: This is a nice stretch with a narrow path above the hill that leads us to the Guadalix River canyon, just above Charco del Hervidero. The views are very good.

A beautiful site! The small lake is perfect for disconnecting and stopping in the shade for a snack. Ideal for families.

Be careful when going down the narrow stairs that have no railings. The ascent is a bit poorly indicated, you have to look a little.

The weir is part of what used to be a reservoir to divert water from the Guadalix River to Madrid back in the early 20th century. Today only ruins remain and it is one of the icons of the well-known Hervidero route.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Loop trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally rated as moderate. While many paths are accessible, it includes significant elevation changes and some sections with steep descents and ascents, particularly near the Hervidero Waterfall, which can be demanding. Good physical fitness and sure-footedness are recommended.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route features diverse terrain, including mostly accessible paths, unpaved sections, and some stretches of road. You'll encounter areas with loose earth and stones, especially on steeper parts. Near the Hervidero Waterfall, there are narrow stairs without railings, requiring careful navigation. The path often follows the Guadalix River.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Loop?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the beautiful Hervidero Waterfall with its two cascades, the impressive Trail to the Guadalix River Canyon, and the historic El Mesto Canal. You'll also see the ruins of the El Mesto Weir and may spot an ancient 10th-century Muslim watchtower, Cerro de la Atalaya, depending on your exact route.

faqs.question: Is the Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible for dogs, it's important to consider the moderate difficulty, varied terrain, and potentially narrow sections. Always keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are capable of handling the distance and elevation changes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring is particularly appealing for this route, as the natural environment is lush and colorful, and the waterfall is likely to be flowing well. Autumn also offers beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, so an early start is advisable, and winters may present colder conditions, though the trail is generally accessible year-round.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information isn't detailed in the provided data, but many hikers start from areas like El Molar. It's recommended to check local parking options near your chosen starting point before your visit.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there is no mention of permits or entrance fees required to hike the Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Loop. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other paths. Notably, it covers about 39% of the Canal de El Mesto trail, 5% of Avenida de Madrid, and 2% of Camino Canal Bajo CYII.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for this trail?
faqs.answer: If you start from El Molar, be prepared for a challenging, steep descent towards the Hervidero Waterfall and a subsequent uphill climb back. While this starting point might offer a less crowded experience, it adds to the physical demand of the hike.

faqs.question: Is the Hervidero Waterfall suitable for swimming?
faqs.answer: The Hervidero Waterfall is known for its crystalline waters and forms a refreshing pool. While it's a beautiful spot, specific information on whether swimming is officially permitted or safe is not provided. Always exercise caution and respect local regulations.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the loop?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy a blend of natural beauty and historical elements. Expect lush, colorful natural environments, especially in spring, with views of the valley, the impressive canyon of the Guadalix River, and the unique
